calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Worth for John Sharpless. Jessica Williams for four residuary Beneficiaries.

Mr. Worth addressed the ancillary Florida and Alabama probate matters stating that there are no orders for final distribution in those matters, that there is nothing to distribute in the Alabama matter, that there is the Gibbs property in Florida and that the only other asset is cash in this estate. Mr. Worth also explained that there is one Attorney for both of the ancillary matters, that compensation/commissions for the administrator is at 2.5% for the Alabama probate and 3% for Florida probate which are based on the value of the assets of those estates, and that those are the numbers that are proposed in the calculations. In addition, Mr. Worth addressed the issue of fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Of Will Contest And For Unconditional Admission Of Will To Probate
Off Calendar May-01-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Worth for John Sharpless (Administrator). Eugene Hom for San Francisco SPCA. Maria Lawless for Julie Reilly, Pam Hunter, J. Alex Bell and Beth Hart. Paul Melbostad for Dan Waldrip.

There is no opposition to the Settlement Agreement by any of the Parties. The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement of Will Contest and for Unconditional Admission of Will to Probate as well as the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement and for Preliminary Distribution (subject to review of the orders). The Court will also sign the Order for Probate pursuant to the Settlement Agreement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne/J. Gomez.

Off Calendar Apr-10-2012 Continued To May-01-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eugene Hom for San Francisco SPCA. Thomas Worth for John Sharpless. Paul Melbostad for and with Danny Waldrip.

The Court noted that Attorney Maria Lawless was not present. The Court expressed its concerns and stated that it is not prepared to go forward without Proof of Service of the underline petitions with the Settlement Agreement to Patsy Hoffman, Zachary Hoffman and James Godwin. Counsel addressed the issue of the Court declaring the one page holographic instrument invalid. All matters were continued to May 1, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of consents or Proofs of Service from/for the above named Parties. Mr. Worth is to advise Ms. Lawless of the Court's concerns.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Andrade/A. Payne.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2880 Jackson Street)
Off Calendar Feb-02-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Worth for Administrator. Paul Melbostad for Danny Walsrip.

Mr. Worth assured the Court that Mr. Hom had no interest in the property, that he was not attending todays hearing and stated that $850,000.00 will go to the SPCA. The sale was confirmed at $630,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). The proceeds of the sale are to be placed into more than one blocked account. Status of filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts was set for February 21, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Melbostad expressed concern about liability issues with the Clayton Street property and distribution of said property. The Amended Petition for Probate and status of the Petition for Approval of The Settlement Agreement were also continued to February 21, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of a petition for a preliminary distribution was also set for that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Judge: Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Melbostad for Dan Waldrip. Eugene Hahn for San Francisco SPCA. Maria Lawless for Contestants.

The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial, (order for the same signed in open court). The Court will schedule a settlement conference and notify the Parties of the place and time.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1918 Divisadero Street, S.F.)
Off Calendar Sep-13-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Worth for John Sharpless.

The sale was confirmed at $1,250,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (order for the same signed in open Court). The proceeds are to be deposited into a blocked account. Status of filing the receipt for the blocked account was set for October 4, 2011 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Will Contest
Off Calendar Jul-26-2011 Continued To Sep-27-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maria Lawless for Beth Hart, Julie Reilly, Pam Hunter and Alex Bell (Contestants). Eugene Hahm for SPCA.

Counsel stated that discovery has been conducted but not completed, that they will meet and confer next week and that they will be ready for a 3 to 4 day trial shortly. The status hearing on the will contest was continued to September 27, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and to send the Parties to a settlement conference or for sending the matter out for trial.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maria Lawless for Beth Hart, Pam Hunter, J. alex Bell and Julia Reilly (Contestants). Eugen Hom for San Francisco SPCA.

Mr. Hom stated that they have engaged in some discovery and that they still need 60-days to do out of state depostions. Ms. Lawless stated that they were both waiting for disovery responses to see how many depositions will be needed. Status of the will contest was continued to July 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lazatin.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Feb-01-2011 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Special Administration Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jan-18-2011 Continued To Feb-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maria Lawless for Contestants. Thomas Worth for John Sharpless.

Ms. Lawless stated that they settled with Danny Waldrip, that they were discussing settlement with the SPCA and that Mr. Hom (Counsel for SPCA) was in the process of discussing the matter with his Client. Mr. Worth asked the Court to consider appointing Mr. Sharpless as Administrator instead of Special Adminstrator with respect to the 1984 will with the cavaet that the one page holographic document would still be at issue for the purpose of dealing with the out of state properties. The Court suggested Mr. Worth submit an ex parte application with notice to Mr. Hom and a proposed order for review. The matter was continued to February 1, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Dec-14-2010 Continued To Feb-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eugene Hom for San Francisco SPCA. Paul Melbostad for and with Dan Waldrip. Thomas Worth for John Sharpless.

Counsel reported that they reached a settlement with regard to the outstanding hours, that the paper work on the settlement was not completed and that there was no settlement reached between the SPCA and the other Contestants. The Petition for Letters of Adminisrtation was continued to February 15, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of further settlement discussions. The Parties agreed to extend the Special Letters with full powers to January 18, 2011. Status of Special Administration of the Estate was also set for January 18, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Continued To Dec-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-13-2010 Continued To Oct-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maria Lawless for Contestants. Paul Melbostad for Dan Waldrip. Eugene Hahm for San Francisco SPCA. Thomas Worth for John Sharpless.

Mr. Melbostad, Ms. Lawless and Mr. Hahm agreed that having a neutral private professional fiduciary who lives here would be a waste of resources. Ms. Lawless stated that she would take care of the procedural matters with regard to the will contest and the issuance of the summons, Mr. Worth stated that he would do publication and Mr. Melbostad stated that he would amend the petition. Counsel was advised to file an ex parte application for the additional requested powers and extension of letters. All Parties are willing to participate in mediation. Mr. Hahm stated that he did not have authority to agree to mediation, that he needed to confer with his Client regarding mediation but that he did not see any obstacles. The matter was continued to October 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Hohm to advise the Court as to his Client's position. The order referring the matter to mediation will be signed at that time. Mediation is to be completed by December 14, 2010 and the matter will also be continued to December 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-08-2010 Continued To Oct-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Worth for John Sharpless. Paul Melbostad for and with Dan Waldrip. Maria Lawless for Julia Reilly, Beth Hart, Pamela Hunter and J. Alex Bell.

Mr. Worth stated that Mr. Sharpless found a second holographic document dated 1984, that the 1984 document is the one they want to admit to the Court, that there are many questions with the earlier document and requested the special powers be extended. Ms. Lawless stated that she was just retained on Friday, that she would like to do research on the new document and that she wanted time to do points and authority, and address their concerns. Mr. Melbostad stated that he prepared a declaration by Mr. Waldrip regarding the holographic documents, that he provided a copy to Counsel and that he would file it. The matter was continued to October 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Documents are to be filed by October 6, 2010. The Special powers were extended to October 13, 2010 (order for the same to be signed).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
